title: Pre-Market Briefing - 2026-05-22
markdown:
```markdown
# Pre-Market Briefing

## 1. Market Tone
- **Mixed-to-positive risk tone** in the prior session, with major indices up modestly: S&P 500 **+0.17%**, Nasdaq **+0.09%**, Dow **+0.55%** (The Motley Fool).
- Pre-market headlines lean **company-specific rather than broad macro-driven**, with several earnings movers and a quantum/AI theme in focus.
- **Interpretation:** This looks like a stock-picker tape more than a directional market regime.

## 2. Macro And Calendar
- **No major macro data or Fed/calendar events were provided in the source.**
- Markets were **closed Monday for Memorial Day** and the email notes it will return Tuesday, May 26 (The Motley Fool).
- **Unclear:** No additional economic releases, policy events, or scheduled catalysts were included.

## 3. Earnings And Company News
- **WDAY**: Rose **11% pre-open** after quarterly results beat expectations; AI demand was strong and annualized revenue from AI solutions is approaching **$500 million** (The Motley Fool).
- **TTWO**: Up **over 6% pre-market** as the quarterly loss narrowed significantly; mobile and sports gaming were solid. **GTA VI remains slated for November** (The Motley Fool).
- **CPRT**: Little changed pre-market after a mixed report; earnings beat, but slower overall growth concerns weighed on the stock (The Motley Fool).
- **ZM**: Up **8% pre-open** after beating estimates and hiking full-year guidance; enterprise growth and adoption of new AI tools were cited (The Motley Fool).
- **IBM**: Closed **12% higher** yesterday on news of U.S. government grants tied to quantum-related projects and a reported **$1 billion** allocation for a chip foundry project (The Motley Fool).
- **GFS**: Closed **15% higher** yesterday alongside IBM on the same government-grant/quantum theme (The Motley Fool).
- **MSFT / NVDA / AMZN / ANTH**: CNBC reports Microsoft is in talks with Anthropic for custom AI chips, with Anthropic diversifying away from Nvidia hardware; this is a reported talk, not a confirmed deal (The Motley Fool citing CNBC).

## 4. Top Movers And Catalysts
- **WDAY** — earnings beat + AI demand / proprietary data engine narrative.
- **ZM** — earnings beat + full-year guidance raise + AI tool adoption.
- **TTWO** — narrower loss + continued anticipation around GTA VI.
- **IBM** — government grants / quantum-related project announcement.
- **GFS** — sympathy move from the same quantum/grant news.
- **MSFT / NVDA / AMZN / ANTH** — headline-level AI compute/chip diversification angle; deal status unclear.

## 5. Ranked Buy Candidates
> Based only on the provided material; no forced picks if the setup is weak.

### 1) **WDAY** — strongest swing candidate
- **Catalyst:** Quarterly beat, AI demand surge, annualized AI solutions revenue near **$500M** (The Motley Fool).
- **Why it might matter for a swing trade:** The move combines **fundamental beat + a visible AI growth narrative**, which can support follow-through if the market rewards quality growth and margin/AI monetization.
- **What would invalidate it:** If the post-earnings gap reverses and the stock loses momentum, or if the market treats the AI story as already priced in.
- **What must be checked before entry:** Pre-market hold, opening range behavior, whether the gap is holding above prior resistance, and whether volume confirms the move.

### 2) **ZM**
- **Catalyst:** Earnings beat, **full-year guidance raised**, strong enterprise growth, and adoption of new AI tools (The Motley Fool).
- **Why it might matter for a swing trade:** Guidance raises can extend post-earnings strength beyond the first reaction, especially if traders focus on renewed growth durability.
- **What would invalidate it:** Failure to hold the gap or quick fade back into the prior range would suggest the market is not buying the improvement.
- **What must be checked before entry:** Gap-and-hold behavior, volume, whether the open holds above key intraday levels, and if the guidance raise is being rewarded rather than sold.

### 3) **TTWO**
- **Catalyst:** Quarterly loss narrowed significantly; mobile and sports gaming were solid; **GTA VI still targeted for November** (The Motley Fool).
- **Why it might matter for a swing trade:** A narrower loss can help sentiment, and GTA VI remains a forward-looking narrative that may keep attention on the name.
- **What would invalidate it:** If the stock cannot sustain the pre-market pop or if the market views the report as only modestly better.
- **What must be checked before entry:** Whether the pre-market move is holding, if there is clean intraday trend structure, and whether volume supports continuation.

## 6. Swing Setup Board
| Ticker | Type | Trigger | Stop / Invalidation | First Target Logic | Sizing Caution | Pass If… |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **WDAY** | Catalyst Continuation | Holds the pre-market gap and reclaims/maintains early session strength | Gap fades and loses opening support / prior resistance | First target is a measured extension off the earnings gap and prior swing resistance area | Post-earnings gaps can be volatile; size smaller than a normal trend trade | Opening action is weak, volume is light, or the gap is immediately sold |
| **ZM** | Catalyst Continuation | Gap holds and price stays above opening range highs | Loss of gap support or failed reclaim after first pullback | Aim for continuation into prior reaction highs if momentum persists | Earnings gap risk is high; avoid oversized entries | Guidance raise is not rewarded and price churns below the open |
| **TTWO** | Reversal Bounce / Catalyst Continuation | Pre-market strength holds through the open and the stock builds higher lows | Breaks back below the pre-market pivot and fails to reclaim | First target is a retest of the pre-market extension zone / prior resistance | The move is more narrative-driven than the others; keep size modest | The open is weak and buyers fail to defend the earnings reaction |
| **IBM** | Risk Watch | Only actionable if it bases after yesterday’s large move | Fails to hold the post-news breakout | Any bounce would need acceptance above yesterday’s range | Large one-day gain raises chase risk | No consolidation; extended from yesterday’s jump |
| **GFS** | Risk Watch | Only actionable if sympathy strength persists with volume | Fades the sympathy move and loses yesterday’s range | Would need a clean hold and continuation over the breakout area | Sympathy names often retrace quickly | No fresh catalyst beyond IBM-linked sympathy |

## 7. Setup Watch
- **WDAY**: Best-looking post-earnings continuation candidate, but needs confirmation that buyers defend the gap.
- **ZM**: Watch for a clean gap-and-go or at least a strong hold above opening support.
- **TTWO**: Trade only if the pre-market strength persists; otherwise it may become a fade setup.
- **IBM / GFS**: Watch for exhaustion after a large move; these may be extended rather than actionable.
- **MSFT / NVDA / AMZN / ANTH**: Only a headline watch right now; the Anthropic chip report is not confirmed as a deal.

## 8. Risk Watch
- **Post-earnings gap fades:** **WDAY**, **ZM**, and **TTWO** all have the risk of opening strong and then giving back gains.
- **Extended sympathy move risk:** **IBM** and **GFS** may be too extended after the quantum/grant reaction.
- **Headline-only risk:** The Microsoft/Anthropic chip discussion is reported as “in talks,” so it should not be treated as a confirmed catalyst.
- **Crowded AI narrative risk:** Several names are trading on AI-related optimism; crowded positioning can increase whipsaw risk.

## 9. Sector And Theme Watch
- **AI / enterprise software:** **WDAY**, **ZM**, and **MSFT**-linked AI chip headlines are the main theme.
- **Quantum / government funding:** **IBM** and **GFS** are tied to the government grant story; the source says nine quantum-related companies were selected.
- **Gaming:** **TTWO** is moving on earnings and the continued GTA VI narrative.
- **Semis / compute:** The Anthropic-Microsoft chip report keeps **NVDA** and custom AI silicon in the conversation, but the source is not definitive.

## 10. Worth Reading
- **Workday earnings and AI world model commentary** (The Motley Fool) — strongest single report for a possible swing continuation.
- **Zoom earnings and guidance raise** (The Motley Fool) — useful for gauging whether growth software is being rewarded again.
- **IBM / quantum government grant story** (The Motley Fool) — important for theme traders, though the move may already be extended.
- **CNBC-reported Anthropic/Microsoft chip talks** as relayed in the email — worth monitoring, but confirm before acting.

## 11. Open Questions
- Will **WDAY** and **ZM** **hold their pre-market gains after the open**, or will the moves fade?
- Is the **IBM / GFS** quantum rally a durable theme or just a one-day headline reaction?
- Is the **TTWO** move strong enough to persist without a broader gaming-sector catalyst?
- How much of the **AI-chip** headline for Microsoft/Anthropic is already priced in, given it is only reported as talks?
- **Unclear:** No macro calendar or market-moving economic releases were included.

## Notable Noise
- The **Microsoft / Anthropic chip** item is preliminary and should be treated as low-confidence until confirmed.
- **IBM** and **GFS** may be more “already moved” than fresh entries.
- The email includes promotional commentary and analyst opinions; useful for context, but not a substitute for price/volume confirmation.
```

AI Generated Morning Read

11. Open Questions

Notable Noise
